samples.xml_description:
<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="state_idle">Idle state.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state_rise">Rise state.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state_down">Down state.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">State of the edge detect.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="combinational_logic" width_description="[1:0]">Next state of the edge detect.</reg>
        <reg id="next_rise" type="combinational_logic">Next value of rise.</reg>
        <reg id="next_down" type="combinational_logic">Next value of down.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. The rising and falling edges is detected on the positive edge of the clock signal clk. If a rising edge is detected, the rise output signal is set to 1. If a falling edge is detected, the down output signal is set to 1. Otherwise, both output signals are set to 0. These output signals are synchronized with the clock and remain set to 1 until the corresponding edge appears again.
    </implementation>
</module>
